coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 8–10 miles, Z2 (130–140 bpm), rolling terrain if available but nothing steep. Yesterday's 12-miler at 141 bpm was solid work; today you dial back hard. You're 85 days out in taper—volume is already cut 56% week-over-week, so the stimulus is retention, not accumulation. Your RHR is 56 (down from 61 avg), which is clean recovery signal. Keep today conversational and use it to shake out the legs before Saturday's final long effort. No vert pushing.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You're sleeping 6.36h per night on a 91.5% efficiency—solid but not surplus. Eat breakfast with real carbs (100–120g) before you run; don't do fasted Z2 work this deep in taper when sleep is moderate. Post-run, hit 30–40g protein and 60g carbs within 30 minutes to anchor glycogen before tomorrow's travel (I assume you're still moving for work). Hydrate to pale urine; at your mileage volume, you're not depleted, but the alpine race won't forgive dehydration prep.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Recovery score is 71—green light, but sleep duration is the limiting factor. Tonight, aim for 7+ hours; taper is where you cash in sleep debt, not rack it up. No sauna, no aggressive massage. If work travel today is heavy, prioritize one nap (20–30 min) in the afternoon over anything else. Cold plunging is unnecessary and counterproductive this week; your nervous system doesn't need additional stressor. Sleep is your biggest training tool right now.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 12 weeks from the start line. This is the week you stop doubting the preparation and start visualizing execution. The Julian Alps 50K is technical and sparse on aid—mental brittleness kills people there, not fitness. Use today's easy run to rehearse your self-talk for the high-elevation sections around km 35–45 when legs are empty. By Saturday, you'll have logged 35+ miles this taper week with controlled intensity; that's enough to confirm readiness without digging the hole deeper.
